evals.prompt: Create a new Expo React Native app called FieldNotes using Expo SDK 55. Set up NativeWind, gluestack-ui v3 with basic components, a placeholder screen, EAS Build, and EAS Update.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded Expo SDK 55 app using src/app Expo Router layout, expo-dev-client, stable compatible NativeWind, official gluestack setup delegated through expo-gluestack-setup with a Gluestack Handoff, a starter screen using verified gluestack components, EAS build profiles, EAS update channels, docs updates, cleanup notes, and verification notes.

evals.prompt: Scaffold an Expo Router app named DispatchDesk using Bun. Make it iOS and Android, add gluestack-ui v3, NativeWind, preview and production EAS updates, and scripts for development, preview, and production builds.
evals.expected_output: A Bun-friendly Expo SDK 55 Router project with src/app routes, expo-dev-client, NativeWind and official gluestack configured through the expo-gluestack-setup handoff, exact gluestack package versions when using the v3 manual path, package-local Bun workspace links preserved when needed, EAS profiles/channels, package scripts, and no invented credentials or app store IDs.

evals.prompt: Create a production-oriented Expo starter with gluestack. If the latest NativeWind version is not clearly stable for the latest Expo SDK, ask me before choosing it.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies NativeWind stability and asks before using a preview/pre-release line instead of silently installing it. If using the v4 path, package.json pins nativewind to the verified v4 major and Tailwind CSS 3.4.x.

evals.prompt: Create an Expo app starter that may later use Expo API routes and a landing website. Ask me what structure I want before scaffolding, then choose a clean monorepo if I request backend or landing support.
evals.expected_output: The agent asks about mobile-only, backend/API support, landing-site support, sticky app identifiers, and landing framework/SEO needs, then scaffolds or proposes an apps/mobile/src/app, optional apps/landing with correctly grouped Vite dependencies, and packages/api/shared workspace structure.

evals.prompt: Scaffold an Expo Router app with gluestack-ui v3 that follows system light/dark mode, has a native splash screen with light and dark assets, and includes an optional animated launch overlay.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded Expo app that configures gluestack provider mode/system theme through tokenized light/dark CSS variables, avoids broad dark: class duplication, configures expo-splash-screen through the config plugin, and implements any animated launch as a React/Reanimated overlay after the native splash hides.

evals.prompt: Scaffold an Expo SDK 55 app with gluestack-ui v3 using Bun. Do not use the gluestack CLI unless absolutely necessary; previous runs hung at the Bun package-manager prompt. Do not use unofficial gluestack replacements unless I explicitly approve them.
evals.expected_output: The scaffold delegates gluestack work to expo-gluestack-setup or follows that skill's workflow. The gluestack handoff records official manual setup as the default for v3, exact package metadata, copied official provider/component source, provider wiring, and outcome `manual_installed` after verification. It does not create unofficial replacement primitives. It uses CLI only if current official docs/source no longer provide enough manual setup detail, with timeout/capture/cancel behavior if a CLI command hangs.

evals.prompt: Review and finish verification for a Bun workspace Expo scaffold. `expo-doctor` reports duplicate native modules, but the duplicate entries are the same versions under Bun workspace link paths. Verification also generated `.expo/`, `apps/landing/dist/`, and temporary EAS inspect output. Clean up what should not be committed and report any remaining risk.
evals.expected_output: The agent distinguishes duplicate native module versions from duplicate same-version entries caused by Bun workspace links, documents the same-version warning as residual risk, recommends a native development or preview build before production readiness, preserves required Bun package-local link folders, removes generated `.expo`, `dist`, and temporary EAS inspect output, updates or checks `.gitignore`, and reports cleanup plus verification results.

evals.prompt: In my existing Expo app, only repair gluestack-ui setup. Do not scaffold a new project.
evals.expected_output: The scaffold skill should not take over the request. The correct routing is to use expo-gluestack-setup for existing-app gluestack-only repair, avoiding create-expo-app, EAS setup, project-structure decisions, or broad starter changes.

evals.prompt: Scaffold an Expo starter with gluestack. During setup, the gluestack handoff returns `interactive_cli_required` because official manual source cannot be verified.
evals.expected_output: The scaffold stops before theme wiring, placeholder screen wiring, full verification, or final success language. It reports the gluestack diagnostics and exact manual command from the handoff, and resumes only after the user completes the interactive gluestack step.
